abbreviation_map.crispr: clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeats
abbreviation_map.kegg: kyoto encyclopedia of genes and genomes

soil_conflicts.armyworm_risk: almost always the English word 'as'; arsenic essentially never occurs. Catastrophic misfire. MUST be removed/disabled for this domain.
used as 'ca.'/'ca' meaning circa/approximately, never calcium. Misfire.
'NA medium' (nutrient agar) and Portuguese 'na'; not sodium nor 'not applicable'. Misfire.
'ND-1' mitochondrial NADH-dehydrogenase gene; not 'not detected'. Misfire.
genuinely cadmium in a few hyperaccumulator/SynCom papers, but off-topic for armyworm biology; low risk but irrelevant. Drop.
'AG 1051' hybrid name and 'AA/AG' genotype; the soil CONTEXT_DISAMBIG could still misfire on the 'crop/field/management' branch. Drop for this domain.
'-Ib-cr' resistance gene suffix and 'PI-Cr' population code; neither chromium nor crop residue. Drop.
'SfNPV-Ar' baculovirus strain code; not arid/aromatic. Drop.
'trypsin PI' (proteinase inhibitor) and 'primary insomnia'; not inorganic phosphate. Drop.
ALSO jasmonic acid here (plant-defense signalling). Safe overlap; can keep but it is plant-side, not core entomology.
'CK' = control check group, not cytokinin. Misfire. Drop.
genus abbreviations ('C. ruficrus') and English; carbon context unlikely but soil CONTEXT_DISAMBIG for 'c' should not be carried over.
sample size 'n', 'non-', Portuguese 'no/na'; not nitrogen. Drop the soil 'n' context rule.
rare and tillage/N-treatment senses absent; drop.
genuinely hectare here ('USD/ha', 'ha of corn'). Safe overlap; keep is acceptable.
genuinely carbon dioxide (elevated-CO2 papers). Safe overlap; keep is acceptable but low value.

rejected_ambiguous.reason: Portuguese reflexive 'se' / selenium / Spodoptera exigua — too polysemous to expand.
English 'its' AND ITS rRNA region (df 439); never expand.
'18.5 SC' (suspension concentrate formulation) vs 'sea cucumber' vs strain codes — unsafe.
Gene Ontology vs English 'go' — borderline; left out to be safe.
'EC' emulsifiable concentrate vs EC50 vs European Commission — unsafe.

integration.summary: Add named domain dictionaries to text_normalization.py and thread a `domain` argument (default 'soil', preserving current behaviour) through normalize_text -> embed._embed/run_embed -> CLI `embedding --domain`.
changes: biblion/text_normalization.py: rename the existing module-level dicts to SOIL_ABBREVIATION_MAP / SOIL_CONTEXT_DISAMBIG; add ARMYWORM_ABBREVIATION_MAP / ARMYWORM_CONTEXT_DISAMBIG from this brief; build a DOMAIN_MAPS = {'soil': (SOIL_*), 'armyworm': (ARMYWORM_*)} registry. Keep module-level ABBREVIATION_MAP/CONTEXT_DISAMBIG = the soil ones as backward-compatible aliases.

biblion/text_normalization.py: change expand_abbreviation_in_context(word, context_terms, abbrev_map=ABBREVIATION_MAP, context_disambig=CONTEXT_DISAMBIG) to take the two maps as params (defaulting to the soil aliases). normalize_text(text, ..., domain='soil') resolves DOMAIN_MAPS[domain] and passes them down; raise a clear error on unknown domain.

biblion/embed.py: add `domain: str = 'soil'` to _embed(...) and run_embed(...); pass it into the two normalize_text(...) calls (line ~90); include it in the manifest stamp as 'embedding_domain'. Update the module docstring note about soil tuning.

biblion/__main__.py: in cmd_embedding pass domain=args.domain into run_embed; add `sem.add_argument('--domain', default='soil', choices=['soil','armyworm'], help='Abbreviation dictionary domain (default soil)')`. --no-normalize still wins (skips expansion entirely).

tests: no existing test imports text_normalization (grep-confirmed), so soil defaults keep all current tests green. Optionally add tests/unit/test_text_normalization.py asserting domain='armyworm' expands 'faw'->'fall armyworm' and that 'as' is NOT touched, and domain='soil' still expands 'cd'->'cadmium'.
integration.why_not_merged_map: A single merged map is unsafe: identical tokens carry opposite meanings across domains. 'as' is arsenic in soil but the English word 'as' (858 docs) in armyworm; 'nd' is 'not detected' vs the ND-1 gene; 'na' is sodium/NA vs nutrient agar; 'ck' is cytokinin vs control check. Merging would either silently corrupt one corpus or require so many context rules that the maps become unmaintainable. Disjoint per-domain dicts selected explicitly are the only safe option.
